name: vectors
context: Supabase Vectors — store embeddings with pgvector, perform similarity search, and build RAG pipelines in Postgres
info: Supabase Vectors uses the pgvector Postgres extension to store, index, and query vector embeddings directly in your database. Enable with `CREATE EXTENSION vector`. Vectors are stored in `vector(dimensions)` columns (e.g., `vector(1536)` for OpenAI ada-002, `vector(3072)` for text-embedding-3-large). Similarity search uses operators: `<=>` (cosine distance), `<->` (Euclidean/L2), `<#>` (negative inner product). Create HNSW or IVFFlat indexes for fast approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. Common pattern: generate embeddings from text via OpenAI/Cohere/local models, store in a vector column, then query with a SQL function via `supabase.rpc()`. This enables RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), semantic search, recommendations, and document similarity. Supabase also provides `supabase.ai.embed()` for generating embeddings from edge functions.

sections.title: Setup and schema
sections.body: Enable pgvector with `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector` in a migration. Create tables with `vector(N)` columns where N matches your embedding model's dimensions. OpenAI text-embedding-3-small = 1536, text-embedding-3-large = 3072, Cohere embed-v3 = 1024. Always create an HNSW index on the vector column for fast similarity search.

sections.title: Similarity search
sections.body: Create a Postgres function that uses `<=>` (cosine distance) to find similar vectors. Return `1 - (embedding <=> query_embedding)` as similarity score (0-1, higher = more similar). Filter by threshold to exclude poor matches. Call from the client via `supabase.rpc('match_documents', { query_embedding, match_threshold, match_count })`.

sections.title: RAG pipeline
sections.body: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: (1) embed the user's question, (2) find similar documents via vector search, (3) build a context string from retrieved documents, (4) send context + question to an LLM. This pattern grounds LLM responses in your specific data. Use match_threshold to filter irrelevant documents. Chunk large documents before embedding.

sections.title: Index types and tuning
sections.body: **HNSW** (recommended): graph-based, excellent recall, no training needed. Parameters: `m` (connections, default 16), `ef_construction` (build quality, default 64). **IVFFlat**: partition-based, faster to build, needs `lists = sqrt(rows)`. Query-time: `SET hnsw.ef_search = N` for accuracy tradeoff. Choose operator class: `vector_cosine_ops` for cosine, `vector_l2_ops` for Euclidean.
